repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
According to https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-what-workflows-do/run-job-variations it doesn't look like the matrix looks quite like we've been assuming it does.

Essentially anything extra in "include" that doesn't contradict something existing is added everywhere. So the `gcc_version: 13` aren't being added as extra subjobs but are being applied to everything that the other fields match.

I think the gist is that we at least need to specify `extra_hash` to the original matrix so that they definitely contradict that and get added as as extra job.

comment_data.body: I think this test was not only that it needs C99 but also that it doesn't need more than C99 by accident. I'd keep it for that.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -132,35 +134,29 @@ jobs:
           # Ubuntu sub-jobs:
           # ================
           # GCC 13 (with broad language standards)
-          - os: ubuntu-22.04
-            python-version: "3.9"
-            backend: c
-            gcc_version: 13
-            extra_cflags: "-std=c99"
-            extra_hash: "-c99"

comment_data.body: Alternatively, we could generally pass this option everywhere in our CFLAGS now.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -132,35 +134,29 @@ jobs:
           # Ubuntu sub-jobs:
           # ================
           # GCC 13 (with broad language standards)
-          - os: ubuntu-22.04
-            python-version: "3.9"
-            backend: c
-            gcc_version: 13
-            extra_cflags: "-std=c99"
-            extra_hash: "-c99"

comment_data.body: Yeah happy to revert that one
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -132,35 +134,29 @@ jobs:
           # Ubuntu sub-jobs:
           # ================
           # GCC 13 (with broad language standards)
-          - os: ubuntu-22.04
-            python-version: "3.9"
-            backend: c
-            gcc_version: 13
-            extra_cflags: "-std=c99"
-            extra_hash: "-c99"

commit.message:
Fully specify CI matrix

According to https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-what-workflows-do/run-job-variations
it doesn't look like the matrix looks quite like we've been
assuming it does.

Essentially anything extra in "include" that doesn't contradict
something existing is added everywhere. So the `gcc_version: 13`
aren't being added as extra subjobs but are being applied to
everything that they match.

I think the gist is that we at least need to specify `extra_hash`
to the original matrix so that they definitely contradict that
and get added as as extra job.
Bump Ubuntu to 24.04444 Please enter the commit message for your changes. Lines starting

Remove C99 - we require it anyway

... so it doesn't have to be a separate test
